Transcrição Completa: 01 Introduction & Overview
[00:00] [Music] how does your company create value it seems like a simple question but in my experience most entrepreneurs have no idea how to answer it so I'm going to ask you again what is your value creation process do you know where value actually rests in your business
[00:20] now if your answer is no or if you're just a little unsure uh then I have good news by the end of this session you will have an answer to this question because our goal for this module is to visually document how your company creates value through a process that we call Value engine mapping
[00:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Value Engine Mapping - Scalable Operating System Framework"
NÚMEROS: Módulo 01
VISUAL: Título do módulo destacado, introdução ao conceito de Value Engines como primeira ferramenta do framework de sistema operacional escalável
[00:45] value engines are the first tool that make up the broader scalable operating system framework and they're also the tool that forms the foundation of the set of algorithms that we'll be documenting over the next few sessions
[01:00] now let me give you the backstory for how we discovered value engines and I want you to tell me if any of this sounds familiar if you've ever kind of been through one of these situations yourself
[01:15] now for as long as I've been in business I've heard that you need to document your company's systems and processes have you heard that right you need to have checklists you need to have standard operating procedures for everything you do right that's what we've all been told
[01:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Traditional Approach - Document Everything"
NÚMEROS: 5 tarefas mais comuns e críticas, 1 mês de projeto
VISUAL: Representação do mandato tradicional de documentação de processos, checklist genéricos, SOPs para todas as atividades
[01:35] and we did that I mean I remember sending out a massive mandate across the entire company declaring that everyone needs to write down and document in clear step-by-step detail their five most common and critical tasks
[01:50] we spent an entire month on this project and we nearly shut down the company in the process I mean it was a really really big deal
[02:00] and when we were finished we had a beautiful binder filled with dozens of checklists that wait for it literally no one ever used
[02:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Failed Documentation Project"
NÚMEROS: 1 mês de trabalho, 0% de uso
VISUAL: Imagem de um binder bonito mas não utilizado, representação visual do desperdício de tempo e recursos
[02:15] and that's not exaggeration by the way none of these checklists that we spent so much time creating were ever used on a day-to-day basis it was one of the single biggest wastes of time in the company's history
[02:30] and I remember being so frustrated because I knew that our people cared I mean heck one of our core values is document and share what you know so I knew the team cared and I knew they wanted to do good work and yet the checklists just weren't getting used why right why Why did no one ever use them when we put so much work into their creation and when everyone agreed that this project was super important
[02:55] well I had to find out so I assembled a companywide meeting to get to the bottom of it right when I pulled the team the answers ranged from you know what Ryan it's just that we already know how to do this stuff or it just takes too long or just down to the hey I forgot
[03:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Why Checklists Failed - Team Feedback"
NÚMEROS: 3 razões principais
VISUAL: Lista de justificativas: "já sabemos fazer isso", "demora muito tempo", "esquecemos", representação de frustração e confusão
[03:20] right now these were all semi-helpful but none of them really felt like the real answer so in a moment of exhausted frustration I remember saying okay you know what screw it how do products get made all right we're a company we make products so how does that happen
[03:40] um and then when you know someone buys one of our products you know what what happens then and and and while we're at it how do we get new customers in the first place right we're a company we got customers how does that happen what's the first step in the process
[04:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Breakthrough Questions"
NÚMEROS: 3 perguntas fundamentais
VISUAL: Layout de três perguntas-chave destacadas: "Como os produtos são feitos?", "O que acontece quando alguém compra?", "Como conseguimos novos clientes?"
[04:05] and I remember starting from there I was like okay customers how do they happen and I was standing in our conference room in front of our whiteboard and I had this like stack of sticky notes in my hand
[04:20] and when someone said something I just wrote it down on a sticky note and I stuck it to the board okay everyone so that's the first step in this process I would ask does everyone agree
[04:30] now invariably uh someone would speak up and say no you know what actually I I think it's this other step that comes first so I'd write that one down uh and I would you know stick that one to the board in front of the other one
[04:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Sticky Note Method"
NÚMEROS: Passo a passo iterativo
VISUAL: Whiteboard com sticky notes sendo organizados, processo visual de mapeamento colaborativo, notas quadradas conectadas por linhas
[04:50] okay great I would say then what happens someone would speak up I'd write down what they said and I would stick it to the right of the previous sticky note and this process continued then what happens then what happens
[05:05] occasionally someone would speak up and say well it depends if they do X then this happens and if they do y then this other thing happens
[05:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Decision Points - The Diamond Shape"
NÚMEROS: 2 caminhos possíveis (X ou Y)
VISUAL: Sticky note girado em formato de diamante representando bifurcação no processo, linhas saindo de cada ponto indicando diferentes caminhos
[05:20] okay I said and I turned the square sticky note on an angle to form a diamond and added lines from each point denoting that there was a simple fork in the road
[05:30] okay let's go down this road for now I said you know assuming someone makes it to this point then what happens then what then what and if they go down this other path because they didn't show up for their appointment or they ask you know for the blue widget instead of the red one my question was the same then what happens right right then what then what
[05:55] eventually we reached a point where everyone agreed that's it right this phase of the customer acquisition or the product fulfillment or the product creation process is done
[06:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Complete Value Engine Map"
NÚMEROS: 12+ sticky notes conectados
VISUAL: Whiteboard completo com aproximadamente uma dúzia de sticky notes interconectados com linhas de marcador, representando fluxo completo de criação de valor
[06:15] when we were finished I just remember looking at the Whiteboard and thinking this is it right this is it this is how we as a company create value
[06:25] now there wasn't much to it it was just a dozen or so sticky notes all connected with you know dry erase mark lines but it was oddly beautiful in its Simplicity
[06:35] it wasn't a single isolated checklist detailing how to perform a single isolated task what we had for the first time ever was a visual representation a map for how we as a company create value by either acquiring a customer or fulfilling on the promises made to that Customer
[06:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What All Businesses Really Do"
NÚMEROS: 3 atividades fundamentais
VISUAL: Três pilares destacados: "Fazemos coisas para vender", "Vendemos as coisas que fazemos", "Entregamos as coisas que vendemos"
[07:00] because let's face it at the end of the day that's kind of all businesses really do is isn't it we make stuff to sell we sell the stuff we make and we deliver the stuff we make to the customer after we've sold it that's it right
[07:15] this simple and admittedly ugly mish mash of sticky notes accomplished something in a matter of minutes that we had failed as a company to accomplish in a month
[07:25] we finally understood how our company acquired customers and served those customers and we finally understood how all those disperate tasks sitting in that stupid freaking binder fit together
[07:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "From Disconnected Checklists to Orchestrated Flow"
NÚMEROS: Minutos vs. 1 mês
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre checklists isolados e desconectados versus fluxo orquestrado e conectado, metáfora de balé coordenado
[07:45] in other words as opposed to thinking about our business as a series of just disconnected checklists we instead saw it as an orchestrated flow where one activity flowed seamlessly to the next like a beautifully coordinated ballet a single visual map of our value creation process let me tell you this changed everything
[08:10] now that we could see the big picture and understand how value flows throughout the organization we could look down on it from a 30,000 foot View and ask of all these tasks and activities represented by all these little sticky notes which ones are truly critical
[08:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "30,000 Foot View - Identifying Critical Tasks"
NÚMEROS: 30,000 pés de altitude, 2-3 tarefas críticas por engine
VISUAL: Vista aérea do mapa de valor, perspectiva de alto nível, identificação de tarefas que realmente movem a agulha
[08:35] which ones actually move the needle which ones do we really need to get right when we asked this question we discovered that really only two or three tasks within a given value engine were truly critical
[08:50] sure the other steps needed to happen but there were always a handful that we absolutely positively had to get right or the entire value engine would fail
[09:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Power Stages - The Critical Few"
NÚMEROS: 2-3 power stages por value engine
VISUAL: Destaque visual de 2-3 etapas críticas dentro do fluxo maior, representação de pontos de falha ou sucesso do sistema
[09:05] we called these critical tasks power stages and we ultimately decided to focus all our documentation efforts on only those handful of stages that we truly had to get right
[09:20] and as for the rest of the checklist we created you know the ones that were still sitting in the binder well they wound up getting filed in the circular filing cabinet which just code in case you don't already know for the trash can
[09:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Fate of Non-Critical Checklists"
NÚMEROS: 100% descartados
VISUAL: Imagem humorística de lixeira (circular filing cabinet), representando eliminação de documentação não-crítica
[09:40] now just to clarify I'm not saying that checklists and Sops aren't important please don't hear that because they absolutely are uh in fact we're going to talk all about building your business Playbook Library which is really just a collection of step-by-step checklists in a later module
[10:00] but in my experience most companies in their attempt to document everything wind up documenting nothing or worse they document everything and then use nothing just like us
[10:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Documentation Paradox"
NÚMEROS: 0% de uso quando se documenta tudo
VISUAL: Paradoxo visual: tentar documentar tudo = documentar nada, ou documentar tudo = usar nada
[10:20] the big mistake that everyone is making is they're getting the process of process documentation out of order which I acknowledge by the way is more than a little bit ironic
[10:30] so let me be clear all right step one is not to document all your company's critical tasks and activities
[10:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Correct Order of Process Documentation"
NÚMEROS: Passo 1 vs. Passo 2
VISUAL: Sequência correta destacada: Passo 1 = Mapear fluxos de criação de valor, Passo 2 = Documentar power stages
[10:45] step one is to map your company's core value creation flows and then and only then can you identify your power stages those truly critical actions and activities that need to be documented and measured
[11:00] but that second step of documenting your power stages that one's for another day today we're going to start with documenting your company's value engines because that's the real first step
[11:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Today's Focus: Value Engine Mapping"
NÚMEROS: Passo 1 de 2
VISUAL: Destaque do objetivo da sessão atual, foco exclusivo em mapeamento de value engines como fundação
[11:20] so let's get into it
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
5 tarefas mais comuns e críticas (mandato inicial de documentação)
1 mês completo gasto no projeto de documentação
0% de taxa de uso dos checklists criados
12+ sticky notes para mapear um value engine completo
2-3 power stages críticos por value engine
30,000 pés de altitude (perspectiva necessária para identificar o crítico)
100% dos checklists não-críticos descartados
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Value Engine Mapping: Processo de mapeamento visual do fluxo de criação de valor da empresa
Método Sticky Note: Técnica colaborativa usando whiteboard e sticky notes para mapear processos
Formato Diamante: Sticky notes girados em 45° para representar pontos de decisão/bifurcação
Perguntas "Then What": Metodologia iterativa de perguntar "e então o que acontece?" para mapear fluxos completos
Power Stages: Identificação das 2-3 etapas verdadeiramente críticas dentro de cada value engine
Vista de 30,000 Pés: Perspectiva de alto nível para identificar o que realmente move a agulha
Scalable Operating System Framework: Sistema maior do qual value engines são a primeira ferramenta
ESTRUTURA:
Três Funções Fundamentais de Negócio:
Fazer produtos para vender
Vender os produtos feitos
Entregar os produtos vendidos
Fluxos de Valor Principais:
Aquisição de clientes
Criação/produção de produtos
Cumprimento/entrega de produtos
Ordem Correta de Documentação:
Passo 1: Mapear value engines (fluxos de criação de valor)
Passo 2: Identificar power stages
Passo 3: Documentar apenas os power stages
Módulo Futuro: Business Playbook Library (coleção de SOPs)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Quase fechamento da empresa devido ao projeto de documentação mal executado
Desperdício significativo de recursos (1 mês de trabalho de toda equipe)
ROI negativo do projeto tradicional de documentação (100% de desperdício)
DURAÇÃO: ~11:30 minutos FORMATO: Aula educacional com apresentação de slides e storytelling
